mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase borrowing costs for corporations and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid quarterly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the unemployment rate threshold fails to separate positive from negative return periods with statistical significance (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling housing starts and rising retail sales indicates a structural shift in consumer demand away from housing toward goods, which historically precedes a credit tightening cycle and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces positive forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when tested out-of-sample.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when industrial production accelerates, or the signal's predictive accuracy vanishes during Fed rate adjustment periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls indicate expanding employment and higher aggregate income, which supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth fails to predict positive forward returns, with >50% of high-growth months followed by negative 20-day returns, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index growth above 3.0% annually triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally in >55% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the inflation threshold shows no statistically significant separation of return distributions.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous deceleration in real GDP growth and rising unemployment confirms a broad economic slowdown, reducing earnings forecasts and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined slowdown signal yields positive forward returns in >50% of instances, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from noise in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Positive industrial production momentum during a late-cycle expansion phase reflects resilient manufacturing demand, which supports short-term equity price appreciation over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when industrial production rises during late-cycle phases, or the signal's predictive power disappears outside the specified regime.
